# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for a MongoDB development environment. Define MongoDB service with exposed ports for application connectivity. Set up volume mounts for data persistence. This is for our local development and testing setup.
# Category: mongodb_exposed_port

version: '3'
services: 
    db_dev:   # name of the DB Service (MongoDB) instance in Docker Compose file using convention names are good practice to follow a naming pattern that would make it easier for others. It helps understand, maintain and isolate your services or applications if needed by tools like Kubernetes
      image: mongo  # we will use the official Mongo DB docker imagelayer 'mongo' in our case when defining this service within Docker Compose file   (replace it with appropriate images according to requirement)   
       ports:        # expose port for application connectivity. Use same syntax as services definitions, and set required exposed ports or range of the ones used by mogodb docker image  here.. You can use any available PORT in your system if not defined otherwise Docker will assign one automatically... e.g., "27018:27017"
         - '36549:27017' # make sure to match with the port number of mongodb container using mongo image  and same is used here as example.. Change it according your needs in case not matching... e.g., "mongoDB_port": "36549"
      volumes:        # setup volume mounts for data persistence where to store the Mongod process inside this container    (replace path with appropriate location)  here as example.. For instance, "/data/db:/data/db", and change it according your needs. Docker will use these directory structure in a way that you've specified
        - 'mongo_volume:/data/db', # make sure to match the volume name which we defined earlier (e.g., "MONGODB") as example.. Replace path with appropriate location  here... e.g.", "/home/ubuntu/mongodb"      Then it should be in below format of:
          '<directory-in-the-host>:/<directory-in-docker>` (you can use relative or absolute paths)   and change the directory according to your requirements.. for example, '/var/lib/mongo'  will map from host machine at /var/lib/mongo in docker container.
      restart: always    # make sure Mongod process runs continually even if an error occurred... here it should be 'always'. If not required please delete or change this line.. e.g., "restart" : ''  (can leave empty)   Here, we are forcing the mongodb service to run indefinitely whenever a Docker restart is triggered via terminal/ssh connection
    web:              # Name of another DB instance defined for usage within docker compose file... here as example.. you can name any services according your requirement. Just ensure that it's unique i.e., no other named service should use this 2nd db with the same names (like above 'db_dev'). Also, make sure to match all ports and volumes definition inside each specific DB defined in docker compose file..
      image: node     # we will still be using Node Js Docker imagelayer here... replace it as per requirement.  e.g., "node" (or whatever suits your requirements)   then change related code according to that, if not same remove or add lines accordingly for each service definition in docker compose file
      depends_on:    # use this feature when you want a specific order of execution ie executing one before the other... Here it should be "db".. And also ensure Mongodb container is up and running by specifying your db (the name defined as 'web' here)  then change related code according to that, if not same remove or add lines accordingly for each service definition in docker compose file
      volumes:       # you should define specific path of the app folders inside this particular instance which will be mapped into Docker container... Here it can have multiple items with relative and absolute paths like 'app:/usr/src'   (make sure they match your project structure)  then change related code according to that, if not same remove or add lines accordingly for each service definition in docker compose file
      ports:         # expose port which should be used by web application.. Here it can have multiple items with any available PORT number like '80'   (make sure they match your project requirements)  then change related code according to that, if not same remove or add lines accordingly for each service definition in docker compose file
      environment:    # define the environmental variable specifics which should be passed inside Docker container... Here it can have multiple items like 'NODE_ENV=development'   (make sure they match your project requirements)  then change related code according to that, if not same remove or add lines accordingly for each service definition in docker compose file
